stories.title: Stabbing suspect yells 'Allahu Akbar' before attacking 2 men in New York: police
stories.summary: A 51-year-old man was arrested Thursday after stabbing two men on New York’s Upper West Side while shouting “Allahu Akbar.” The victims, an Asian man and a Jewish man, were hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries and are expected to survive. Police are investigating the attacks as a possible hate crime while assessing mental health factors in the suspect’s motive.

stories.title: Dillon Dingler, James Outman deliver in clutch in Tigers' comeback win
stories.summary: The Detroit Tigers secured a 4-3 comeback win over the Kansas City Royals at Comerica Park, with Dillon Dingler delivering a clutch two-out RBI to break the tie and James Outman contributing a crucial swing. The Tigers trailed 2-0 after six innings but rallied to improve their July record to 12-5 and their overall record since June 1 to 27-16. The team will look to build on this momentum in the second game of the weekend series against the Royals.

stories.title: Why Tom Hiddleston can’t stop thinking about Pompeii
stories.summary: Tom Hiddleston, a classical studies graduate and actor, explores the ancient Roman city of Pompeii in a new three-part series, tracing the stories of three real-life residents on the day Mount Vesuvius erupted in A.D. 79, killing thousands and preserving the city in time. The series, hosted by Hiddleston, delves into new insights into ancient Roman life, guided by experts and featuring scripted drama and documentary storytelling. This project marks a new creative venture for Hiddleston, who previously collaborated with Kevin Wright on the successful Disney+ series Loki.

stories.title: Jerry O’Connell addresses brutal Giuliana Rancic breakup more than a decade after she blasted him in memoir
stories.summary: Jerry O'Connell has broken his silence on his 2004 breakup with Giuliana Rancic, confessing he doesn't recall his exact wording but acknowledging he may have said something similar to what Rancic described in her 2015 memoir, "Going Off Script". The "Jerry Maguire" star, 52, praised Rancic for using colorful dialogue in her book, which included a brutal description of their split. The couple's breakup occurred more than 20 years ago, with Rancic releasing her tell-all memoir over a decade later.

stories.title: AUSL postseason is here! Cascade face Bandits in play-in game (9 ET, ESPN)
stories.summary: The 2026 Athletes Unlimited Softball League postseason has begun with the Chicago Bandits facing off against the Portland Cascade in a win-or-go-home play-in game on Thursday at 9 p.m. ET on ESPN, with the winner advancing to the AUSL Championship against the top-seeded Utah Talons. The AUSL Championship series will begin on Saturday at 1 p.m. ET on ABC, marking the first time a professional softball game has been televised on the network.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will spend his first day in office working from the new No 10 North branch in Manchester, where he will chair a meeting of the National Economic Council, a cabinet committee established in 2008 to address economic challenges across the UK. The meeting will bring together senior ministers and regional mayors to focus on devolution and local economic growth, with up to 300 civil servants expected to join the new site by the end of next year. This marks a significant shift in power from Whitehall and Westminster to regional areas, as the prime minister aims to give more attention and investment to communities that have felt forgotten.

stories.title: Creepy Twins, Religious Fanaticism and a Loaded Gun Collide in Tense Chamber Drama ‘Godhead’ — Fantasia
stories.summary: A rural Catholic priest is being visited by two bizarre twins who claim to be part of a prophecy and need his help to fulfill it, but they also bring a gun to the meeting, adding to the tension. The film, "Godhead," premiered at the Fantasia Festival in Montreal, where it received enthusiastic reviews for its masterful blend of faith, sex, lies, and discomfort. The director, Mark H. Rapaport, aimed to challenge authority figures and leave audiences with difficult questions.

stories.title: 'Big Brother' says goodbye to Traverse City pickleball coach
stories.summary: Rome Seymour, a pickleball coach from Traverse City, has become the second player to be voted off the CBS reality show "Big Brother" this season, with a near-unanimous 12-1 vote. Seymour was evicted despite his efforts to save himself in the BB Block Buster competition, where he was narrowly outperformed by former "RuPaul's Drag Race" standout Jason De Puy. Seymour has taken his early exit graciously, stating that he has no hard feelings towards his fellow houseguests.

stories.title: ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Co-Creator Bill Prady Explains the Science Behind the Show’s Multiverse
stories.summary: A post-apocalyptic Pasadena has been ravaged by zombie hands, giant moths, and reality tears, with the only functioning business being a comic book shop run by Stuart Bloom. The show's multiverse is attributed to a quantum interference device built by "The Big Bang Theory" characters Leonard, Sheldon, and Howard, which has altered reality. Regular Stuart and his companions must now find the device at Cal Tech to potentially save the universe from further destruction.

stories.title: ’72 Hours’ Review: Kevin Hart Tries to Be Down With the Kids in a Mediocre ‘Hangover’ Knockoff
stories.summary: Kevin Hart stars in the mediocre comedy "72 Hours," a hangover-inspired film directed by Tim Story and written by Jon Hurwitz and Hayden Schlossberg, which attempts to revive the crime subplot formula that has often ruined even the most average comedies. The movie, a retread of the "Hangover" franchise, failed to impress critics despite its all-star cast. The film's lackluster performance is a reminder that even the most promising comedies can fall short of expectations.

stories.title: YG vs. The Game: VERZUZ — Compton Forever
stories.summary: Rappers YG and The Game faced off in a VERZUZ battle on July 23, with YG delivering a 22-song setlist that included hits like "Toxic" and "Godzilla". The event, which celebrated the artists' impact on Compton culture, featured live voting from fans, with over 1 million votes cast in real-time. The VERZUZ battle marked a historic moment in hip-hop, bringing together two legendary artists for a night of music and celebration.

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
stories.summary: The US is imposing tariffs ranging from 10% to 12.5% on around 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, EU, Canada, Japan and India, over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour imports. The new duties come into effect on Friday, replacing a temporary 10% tax on foreign goods that expired earlier this year. This move marks the latest escalation in the global trade war, with the US seeking to address human rights abuses and distortive trade practices.

stories.title: UK complacent about war threat, warns defence boss
stories.summary: The UK's defence spending is insufficient to counter the growing threat of foreign attack, warns Dr Charles Woodburn, boss of BAE Systems, with the level of threat being the highest he's seen in his lifetime. The country is not far from the use of autonomous lethal weapons by countries that may not follow the UK's policy of maintaining human control. To meet the government's commitment to spend 3.5% of GDP on defence by 2035, much more investment will be needed.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: A Singapore court has ruled that 19 individuals are guilty of baby trafficking, with at least 34 babies being sold for adoption, 12 of whom were sent to Singapore. The convicted traffickers are now awaiting sentencing, while the fate of the babies remains uncertain, with adoptive parents in Singapore awaiting a decision on whether they can keep their children. The Indonesian government has yet to confirm whether the babies will be returned to Indonesia.

stories.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
stories.summary: A record-breaking heatwave in England has left many feeling anxious, with a 36-year-old stay-at-home mother in Cheltenham sharing her fears on social media, garnering over 18,000 likes and 1,700 reposts. According to a 2022 study from the University of Bath, approximately 4.6% of people in the UK experience climate anxiety, a form of mental distress recognized by professional bodies and the NHS. As temperatures continue to soar, experts warn that managing heatwave anxiety is crucial to prevent feelings of helplessness.

stories.title: Scientists find building blocks of birdsong's 'spectacular diversity'
stories.summary: Scientists have discovered the fundamental building blocks of birdsong's spectacular diversity, identifying eight key types of sound, or "motifs", that are combined in various ways to create the complex songs of over 3,000 bird species worldwide. These motifs include trills, whistles, chaotic notes, and harmonies, which are found in birdsong across Asia, Africa, and the Amazon. The researchers' findings provide a new understanding of the global patterns and variations in birdsong, shedding light on one of nature's most sophisticated forms of sound-based communication.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: China's Moonshot AI has allegedly stolen capabilities from top US artificial intelligence model Anthropic's Fable AI, according to US President Donald Trump's Science and Technology adviser Michael Kratsios, who claims Moonshot used a process called distillation to extract answers from a stronger model. The stolen capabilities were used to develop Moonshot's K3 model, which has been touted as a rival to top US technology. The US government has information suggesting Moonshot also used Nvidia's restricted servers to train its models.

stories.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
stories.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

stories.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
stories.summary: A US health panel has narrowly recommended easing restrictions on several peptides, including BPC-157, TB-500 and KPV, despite warnings from government scientists that their safety and effectiveness have not been proven. The panel's 8-6 vote, with one abstention, is not binding but may influence the health secretary's decision to overturn FDA restrictions. If the FDA follows this advice, telehealth companies are expected to increase marketing of these peptides for wellness and cosmetic uses.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890m by the EU for allegedly favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals, the first major action under the EU's Digital Markets Act. The Commission argued this limited consumer choice and gave Google's services an unfair advantage, with the fine comprised of two separate breaches of the act. Google will now have to strip away features and dismantle safety protections on its services to comply with the EU's requirements.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Hull KR inflicted more derby misery on Hull FC, winning 28-20 in a Super League match that saw the away side extend their winning run to nine games against their city rivals. The struggling Hull FC, who have now lost six straight matches, had taken an early 12-0 lead but were unable to hold on as Hull KR battled back to secure a crucial victory. This win moves Hull KR back into the play-off positions in fifth place.

stories.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
stories.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

stories.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
stories.summary: The Commonwealth Games officially kicked off with a spectacular opening ceremony at the venue in Glasgow, Scotland, featuring a rousing rendition of Scotland's unofficial anthem, "Yes Sir, I Can Boogie," performed by singer Callum Beattie, which got the Scottish athletes dancing in their seats. The ceremony also included a theatrical performance billed as "a love letter to Glasgow and the Commonwealth," and a lively display by the Scotland team, who entered the arena in their white shirts and kilts to a thunderous applause. The event drew a capacity crowd of around 10,000 people.

stories.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
stories.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.

stories.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
stories.summary: Microsoft has responded to allegations that LG monitors are installing McAfee ads on Windows systems without user permission, a practice that has been ongoing for years and has raised concerns about user privacy. The LG Monitor App Installer, which is available on the Microsoft Store, allows the company to collect sensitive user data, including geolocation and online activity. Microsoft has yet to comment on whether it will take action to address this issue, but the incident has sparked questions about the permissions granted to peripherals' companion apps.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.
